select_when: Customers are adopting, switching, or abandoning solutions for reasons that feature comparisons alone do not explain.
The team needs to clarify the real progress the user is trying to make before choosing product scope, messaging, or go-to-market.
A roadmap is organized around attributes or internal categories even though the real lift depends on understanding the outcome being hired for.
The next move should separate the functional job from surface wants, channel feedback, or the team's preferred solution story.
avoid_when: Teams are calling every preference a job and skipping the discipline of distinguishing true progress from surface wants or internal strategy wishes.
The model is being over-applied as a hammer when the real issue is not user progress but another structural problem outside the customer-job frame.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model centered on outcome-focused customer progress, anti-feature obsession, and clear job definition before roadmap or messaging decisions.
donor_drops: Did not preserve broader design-thinking sprawl because the raw source is stronger on identifying the real job and resisting feature-first reasoning.
open_questions: The output type compresses a source that also spans pivots, empathy work, and communication simplification.

select_when.note: The raw source is explicit on switching behavior, real progress, and the need to clarify the job before product and go-to-market choices.

avoid_when.note: The anti-activation conditions are grounded in source warnings against calling every preference a job and using JTBD as a hammer.

input_type.note: The source clearly activates on customer-progress and purpose problems, while the typed input remains a normalized routing label.

output_type.note: The source supports clearer job framing and outcome definition; the output label compresses that into a reusable artifact.

reasoning_types.note: Jobs-to-be-done is strongest as diagnostic and systems-aware reframing of customer progress.
